audit_items.proof_trace: The entire syncio.c file (146 lines, 3 functions) contains zero free() calls and zero heap allocations (confirmed via Grep). All three functions — syncWrite, syncRead, syncReadLine — operate exclusively on caller-provided buffers passed as pointer parameters; they only read from or write to those pointers but never free them. The UAF/double-free invariant holds vacuously over the empty set of free() calls in this file.
